perturbation.original_text: 1. Definitions. Any capitalized term used but not defined in this Amendment will have the meaning set forth for that term in the Non-Competition Agreement or the Master Framework Agreement, dated September 30, 2016, by and between the Parties (the "Master Framework Agreement").
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definitions. Any capitalized term used but not defined in this Amendment will have the meaning set forth for that term exclusively in this Amendment. The definitions within the Non-Competition Agreement or the Master Framework Agreement, dated September 30, 2016, by and between the Parties (the "Master Framework Agreement") shall not be applicable.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition section states that undefined capitalized terms will refer to the Non-Competition Agreement or the Master Framework Agreement. The change explicitly states that those external definitions are *not* applicable. This will create uncertainty if a capitalized term is used in the Amendment but only defined in the Master Framework Agreement or Non-Competition Agreement; it becomes unclear what the term means in the context of the amendment. This contradicts section 4 which states that except as set forth in the amendment, the Non-Competition Agreement will continue in full force and effect in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Continuation. This Amendment will apply and be effective only with respect to the provisions of the Non- Competition Agreement specifically referred to herein. Except as otherwise set forth in this Amendment, the Non-Competition Agreement will continue in full force and effect in accordance with its terms. 1
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that definitions from the Non-Competition Agreement and Master Framework Agreement are *not* applicable, which directly contradicts section 4. Section 4 states that 'Except as otherwise set forth in this Amendment, the Non-Competition Agreement will continue in full force and effect in accordance with its terms.' If the Non-Competition agreement continues in full force, then its definitions should apply unless explicitly stated otherwise. By excluding those definitions, the Amendment contradicts the original agreement's continuation, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Solicitation. Section 4 of the Non-Competition Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "Term. This Agreement will become effective on the Effective Date, and will continue until the expiration of the "Sales Term" as that term is defined in the Sales Dealer Agreement dated as of August 16, 2017 between Vivint and Vivint Solar Developer, LLC (the "Term")."
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Solicitation. Section 4 of the Non-Competition Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "Term. This Agreement will become effective on the Effective Date, and will continue for a period of 1 year, irrespective of any definition provided for the "Sales Term" in the Sales Dealer Agreement dated as of August 16, 2017 between Vivint and Vivint Solar Developer, LLC (the "Term"). At the end of the year, the agreement will renew automatically for another year unless either party provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to the renewal date."
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the agreement will continue for a period of 1 year and automatically renew. This directly contradicts the original text which linked the term to the 'Sales Term' defined in the Sales Dealer Agreement. By disregarding the definition and introducing a fixed term with automatic renewal, the amendment creates uncertainty about the agreement's actual duration. Also, this contradicts section 4, since section 4 states that Except as otherwise set forth in this Amendment, the Non-Competition Agreement will continue in full force and effect in accordance with its terms, but here, it is changing the terms and saying it doesn't matter what the Sales Term in the Dealer agreement says.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Continuation. This Amendment will apply and be effective only with respect to the provisions of the Non- Competition Agreement specifically referred to herein. Except as otherwise set forth in this Amendment, the Non-Competition Agreement will continue in full force and effect in accordance with its terms. 1
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a fixed 1-year term with automatic renewal, irrespective of the 'Sales Term' defined in the Sales Dealer Agreement. This directly contradicts Section 4, which states that "Except as otherwise set forth in this Amendment, the Non-Competition Agreement will continue in full force and effect in accordance with its terms." The amendment alters the term of the agreement, contradicting the provision that the Non-Competition Agreement should remain in full force according to its original terms. This creates a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction as the term is effectively redefined in a way incompatible with the original agreement's incorporation by reference, leading to uncertainty about the agreement's duration and enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Master Framework Agreement. This Amendment is governed by the Master Framework Agreement, including, without limitation, the provisions of Sections 4 (Confidentiality) and 6 (Miscellaneous) of the Master Framework Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Master Framework Agreement. This Amendment is not governed by the Master Framework Agreement. Sections 4 (Confidentiality) and 6 (Miscellaneous) of the Master Framework Agreement do not apply to this amendment.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated that the amendment *is* governed by the Master Framework Agreement, specifically mentioning confidentiality and miscellaneous sections. This change directly contradicts that by stating that the amendment is *not* governed by it and that those sections do not apply. This creates a clear in-text contradiction concerning whether the confidentiality provisions of the Master Framework Agreement apply to this amendment, creating legal ambiguity and potentially affecting the confidential exchange of information between parties related to the amendment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Master Framework Agreement. This Amendment is not governed by the Master Framework Agreement. Sections 4 (Confidentiality) and 6 (Miscellaneous) of the Master Framework Agreement do not apply to this amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Amendment is *not* governed by the Master Framework Agreement, and Sections 4 and 6 do not apply. However, the contradicted text states the same thing. This is contradictory to the original text which stated that the amendment *is* governed by the Master Framework Agreement, and Sections 4 and 6 do apply. This creates a direct in-text contradiction concerning the applicability of the confidentiality provisions of the Master Framework Agreement to this amendment.
